weather.summary: Saturday: 12°C, overcast clouds. Sunday: 14°C, few clouds

metadata.greeting: This weekend is packed with fun activities for your little one! There's a Mr. Men and Little Miss takeover at Battersea Power Station with a treasure hunt, and The Very Hungry Caterpillar Show is at Blackheath Halls, bringing Eric Carle's beloved books to life with puppets. Plus, several museums offer free entry and engaging exhibits perfect for toddlers!

activities.title: Tiny Planet
activities.address: 43 White Horse Road, Limehouse, London E1 0ND
activities.description: A performance for children.
activities.whyItWorks: Engaging and affordable theatre experience for young children.

transport.fromHackney: Take the DLR to Limehouse station, then a short walk.
transport.tip: Check showtimes in advance as availability varies.

activities.title: Mr. Men Little Miss Takeover
activities.description: Step into the vibrant world of Mr. Men/Little Miss through a series of interactive activities, including a treasure hunt where families can use a special map to discover familiar characters.
activities.whyItWorks: Interactive and engaging treasure hunt suitable for a wide range of ages.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to Victoria Station, then the bus to Battersea Power Station.
transport.tip: Check the Battersea Power Station website for specific event times.

activities.title: Museum of Childhood
activities.description: Explore collections of toys from different eras and participate in various activities and workshops designed for children.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a wide variety of toys and activities that can stimulate a 3-year-old's imagination.

transport.fromHackney: Take the bus or Overground to Bethnal Green.
transport.tip: Check the museum's website for workshop schedules.

activities.title: Spitalfields City Farm
activities.description: A city farm with a community feel, featuring animals like goats, pigs, and chickens. Check their website for special Easter activities.
activities.whyItWorks: Kids love animals, and this farm is easily accessible in East London.

transport.fromHackney: Take the bus to Brick Lane.
transport.tip: Check for special events on their website.

activities.title: Biodiversity Playground
activities.description: A small, beautifully designed wooden playground with a pond theme, perfect for toddlers.
activities.whyItWorks: Ideal for toddlers with its small size and engaging design.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube or bus to Stratford.
transport.tip: Good for a quick play session.

activities.title: Museum of London Docklands
activities.description: Discover the history of London's waterfront with interactive exhibits. Also features a soft play area.
activities.whyItWorks: Interactive exhibits and a soft play area are perfect for toddlers.

transport.fromHackney: Take the DLR to Canary Wharf or West India Quay.
transport.tip: Sit at the front of the DLR for a 'driver's seat' experience.

activities.title: The Everywhere Bear
activities.description: Stage adaptation of Julia Donaldson's story brought to life with rhyme, original music and puppets.
activities.whyItWorks: Puppets and music engage young children in this adaptation of a well-known story.

transport.fromHackney: Take the bus to Angel, then a short walk.
transport.tip: Check showtimes in advance.

activities.title: Old School Rooms
activities.description: A community space offering various activities for young children and families.
activities.whyItWorks: Provides a flexible and accessible space for young children.

transport.fromHackney: Take the bus to Clapton.
transport.tip: Check local listings for specific events.

activities.title: Natural History Museum
activities.description: Explore dinosaurs, fascinating creatures, and interactive exhibits.
activities.whyItWorks: Dinosaurs and interactive exhibits capture the imagination of a 3-year-old.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to South Kensington.
transport.tip: Visit the dinosaurs first as it gets crowded.

activities.title: Science Museum
activities.description: Explore interactive exhibits on science, technology, and space. The Pattern Pod is great for younger kids.
activities.whyItWorks: Interactive displays and the Pattern Pod provide a fun, hands-on experience for toddlers.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to South Kensington.
transport.tip: Check out the Wonderlab (paid entry) for even more hands-on exhibits.

activities.title: Sky Garden
activities.description: Visit London's highest public garden with panoramic city views.
activities.whyItWorks: Provides a unique and visually stimulating experience for young children.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to Monument station.
transport.tip: Book tickets online in advance as they are required, even though it's free.

activities.title: Vauxhall City Farm
activities.description: An accessible city farm with animals, gardens, and activities.
activities.whyItWorks: Close encounters with farm animals are perfect for toddlers.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to Vauxhall.
transport.tip: Check for special events on their website.

activities.title: Diana Memorial Playground
activities.description: A pirate ship-themed playground with a sensory trail.
activities.whyItWorks: The pirate ship theme and sensory elements are highly engaging for toddlers.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to Notting Hill Gate or Queensway.
transport.tip: Can get crowded, arrive early.

activities.title: Holland Park Adventure Playground
activities.description: While aimed at older kids, it provides more excitement than the toddler playground in Holland Park.
activities.whyItWorks: Provides a more adventurous play experience for slightly older toddlers.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to Holland Park.
transport.tip: Supervise closely as it's designed for older children.

activities.title: Coram's Fields
activities.description: Seven acres dedicated to children, with an adventure playground, sand pits, a paddling pool (seasonal), and a city farm.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a variety of activities that cater to different interests, including play equipment, water play, and animals.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to Russell Square or King's Cross.
transport.tip: Adults only allowed if accompanying a child.

activities.title: Golders Hill Park Zoo
activities.description: A small, free zoo in Hampstead Heath with rare species of birds and animals.
activities.whyItWorks: Free entry and a variety of animals make it a great option for a day out.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube and bus to Golders Green.
transport.tip: Combine with a visit to Hampstead Heath.

activities.title: Lindt Gold Bunny Hunt
activities.description: Explore the palace grounds looking for gold bunny statues, finishing with a chocolate treat.
activities.whyItWorks: Combines outdoor exploration with a sweet reward.

transport.fromHackney: Take the train to Hampton Court from Waterloo.
transport.tip: Go early in the day to avoid crowds.

activities.title: Kew Gardens – Octonauts Above and Beyond
activities.description: An Octonauts-themed trail through Kew Gardens with missions inspired by the popular series.
activities.whyItWorks: Combines outdoor exploration with a familiar character.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to Kew Gardens.
transport.tip: Allow ample time to explore the gardens.

activities.title: Zoonormous Egg Hunt
activities.description: Explore London Zoo on a special Easter egg hunt, searching for giant Easter eggs hidden around the park.
activities.whyItWorks: Combines a visit to the zoo with an exciting Easter activity.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to Regent's Park.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance.

activities.title: The Very Hungry Caterpillar Show
activities.description: An adaptation of Eric Carle's books brought to life by more than 75 puppets.
activities.whyItWorks: Puppets and familiar stories engage young children.

transport.fromHackney: Take the train to Blackheath.
transport.tip: Check showtimes in advance.

activities.title: British Library's Fairy Tales Exhibition
activities.description: An interactive exhibition exploring enchanted lands, magical creatures, and iconic characters through books, costumes, puppets, and artwork.
activities.whyItWorks: Interactive elements and familiar fairy tales will capture the imagination.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to King's Cross or Euston.
transport.tip: Go early to avoid crowds.

activities.title: Paddington the Musical
activities.description: The world's most beloved bear comes to the stage in this musical adaptation.
activities.whyItWorks: A popular character in a family-friendly musical.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to Charing Cross or Embankment.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance as it is popular.

activities.title: REPLAY: A Limitless Recycled Playground
activities.description: A play space made out of repurposed materials.
activities.whyItWorks: Encourages creativity and play with unusual materials.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to Waterloo or Embankment.
transport.tip: Check Southbank Centre website for opening times.

activities.title: Cutty Sark Easter Egg Trail
activities.price: Included with entry to the ship
activities.description: Take part in an Easter Egg Trail throughout the historic ship. Discover fascinating facts about Cutty Sark's epic voyages, while you explore the decks — and keep your eyes peeled for the hidden eggs. Find them all and unscramble a secret nautical code to claim a free chocolatey treat.
activities.whyItWorks: Combines a historical visit with a fun Easter activity.

transport.fromHackney: Take the DLR to Cutty Sark.
transport.tip: Perfect for slightly older children, but still fun for younger ones with assistance.
